Roads between monuments are poorly lit at night. Multiple travellers report near-misses on scooters. Always wear a helmet, drive before dark, avoid riding under the influence. Carry passport/ID in case of police checks.

reports.title: Poor sanitation near ruins — GI illness risk from street food
reports.place: Hampi Bazaar stalls, temporary food vendors near monuments
reports.desc: Foreign tourist decline report (Nov 2025, Travel & Tour World) cited poor sanitation as a key problem at Hampi. Temporary food stalls near monuments have no running water for hand washing. GI illness from street food is a documented risk. Stick to seated restaurants, ensure food is cooked freshly in front of you, and drink only sealed bottled water.

reports.title: Drone flying illegal at UNESCO heritage site — equipment confiscated
reports.place: All of Hampi UNESCO World Heritage site
reports.desc: Flying drones near or over Hampi's protected monuments is prohibited under ASI regulations. Tourists have had drones confiscated. No permit system exists for casual tourist drone use here. Leave your drone at home.

reports.title: Sharp foreign tourist decline — some services unreliable
reports.desc: Foreign tourist numbers crashed from 19,838 (2023–24) to 3,818 (Oct 2024 YTD) — over 80% drop, per Travel & Tour World (Nov 2025). Local guides, guesthouses and cafés serving foreign tourists have reduced staff and services. Check current operating status of your planned accommodation before travelling. Don't assume all pre-2024 services are still running.

reports.title: Airport taxi mafia at Ngurah Rai arrivals
reports.place: Ngurah Rai International Airport — arrivals exit
reports.desc: Unofficial drivers swarm the arrivals exit quoting 3× the fair price. Legitimate fare to Seminyak is Rp 150k–250k pre-booked; touts demand Rp 500k+. Use the Grab/Gojek waiting room inside the terminal instead.

reports.title: Fake Blue Bird taxi with look-alike logo
reports.desc: Cars painted light blue with near-identical 'Blue Bird' logos are fakes. They refuse the meter and charge 2–3×. Real Blue Bird has 'Blue Bird Group' on the side, driver in uniform, vehicle number on dash. Download the official Blue Bird app to book direct.

reports.title: Broken meter taxi switch mid-ride
reports.desc: Driver agrees to meter, then claims it's broken mid-journey and demands an inflated fixed price. Agree on meter before entering; if the meter suddenly 'breaks', end the ride. Use Grab or Gojek for fixed app pricing.

reports.title: Money changer fast-fold short-count trick
reports.place: Street kiosks — Kuta strip, Jl. Laksmana Seminyak
reports.desc: High advertised rates lure you in, then the cashier palms notes in a fast fold during counting. Viral TikTok 2025 (1.9 M views) caught it on camera at an Ubud counter. Use only licensed operators: PT Central Kuta or PT Dirgahayu. Verify at moneychangerbali.com (Bank Indonesia portal).

reports.title: Money changer 'change for small notes' distraction
reports.place: Kuta strip near Poppies Lane / Centro Mall
reports.desc: Changer gives a pile of small (Rp 20k/50k) notes, counts them, then asks you to find change. While you look in your wallet, a wedge of notes disappears. Reported on Tripadvisor Bali forum. Always be last to count; never look away.

reports.title: ATM 'broken' sign redirects you to a skimmed machine
reports.place: Commonwealth Bank ATM, Seminyak supermarket area
reports.desc: Scammer places a handwritten 'broken' sign on a working ATM to redirect tourists to a nearby compromised machine fitted with a card-skimming device. Reported by an Australian tourist on the 'Bali Bogans' Facebook group. When confronted, the man kept replacing the sign.

reports.title: ATM card skimming by Eastern European gangs
reports.place: ATM galleries — Kuta, Seminyak tourist zones
reports.desc: Bulgarian nationals arrested for fitting card-skimmer sleeves on ATMs in tourist areas, found with Rp 78 million. A Ukrainian woman deported after a 2-year sentence for the same. Use ATMs inside bank branches only (BCA, Mandiri, BNI, BRI). Never use standalone ATM galleries or mini-market ATMs.

reports.title: Fake 'temple guardian' demands mandatory donation at Besakih
reports.desc: Men posing as 'temple guardians' intercept tourists after entry and say a guide is mandatory plus a donation. They show a book with entries up to $100. Everything — guide, sarong, shuttle — is already included in the Rp 150k entry ticket. Real priests never ask for cash. Report pushy individuals at the info desk.

reports.title: Women force sarongs on tourists, then demand payment
reports.desc: Groups of women rush arriving tourists and physically tie sarongs/belts on them before they can refuse, claiming a 'special ceremony' requires extra clothing. Visitors who arrived in traditional temple dress still get targeted. They've been reported following tourists for hundreds of metres demanding payment.

reports.title: Fake priest blessing at Uluwatu demands cash
reports.desc: Men claiming to be priests approach tourists for 'blessings' and then demand payment. Real priests don't solicit tourists. Also: Uluwatu monkeys systematically snatch phones, wallets, and sunglasses — studied by University of Lethbridge as a barter economy. Ransom: Oreo cookies.

reports.title: Forced Canang Sari offering purchase at temple entrance
reports.desc: Women claim tourists MUST purchase Canang Sari (religious offerings) for Rp 100k before entering. The price drops to Rp 50k if you walk away. Completely false. Tourists don't participate in worship; only a sarong is needed and it's provided in the ticket price.

reports.title: Scooter rental pre-existing damage claim
reports.place: Small rental shops islandwide — Canggu, Seminyak, Kuta
reports.desc: Return scooter and the rental agent 'discovers' damage you didn't cause, demanding Rp 500k+. Some shops use duplicate keys to 'steal' the scooter while parked, then charge you. Photograph every scratch and dent before riding. Get damage noted on the rental agreement. Use reputable companies (Bikago has branches islandwide).

reports.title: Roadside fake-mechanic scam on scooter riders
reports.place: Remote roads between Tulamben and Kintamani
reports.desc: Two men approach broken-down or stopped riders, offer help, take the bike to a 'mechanic friend', and return claiming brake pads were replaced — demanding Rp 1.4 million. Rusty old pads shown as 'evidence'. When refused, the men became aggressive and stole the scooter key. Report to Polisi Pariwisata (Tourism Police).

reports.title: Police bribe extortion from scooter riders
reports.place: Jl. Petitenget / Jl. Batu Belig, Seminyak; also Ubud roads
reports.desc: Officers stop tourists on scooters for minor or invented violations (mirror, license) and demand on-the-spot cash 'fine'. International driving permit is required by law in Bali; without it the fine is real (Rp 1.5 million). Carry a valid IDP. For bogus fines, ask for the official ticket and station number — many drop it.

reports.title: Drug peddlers acting as police informants
reports.desc: Locals offer magic mushrooms, ecstasy, or cocaine in tourist zones. Many work with local police: you buy, police appear, you're arrested. Indonesia has the death penalty for drug trafficking and long jail terms for possession. Several foreigners have been executed. Decline all offers, no exceptions.

reports.title: Methanol-laced cocktails causing blindness and death
reports.place: Unlicensed bars, cheap warung cocktail menus, bootleg arak
reports.desc: Home-distilled arak spiked with methanol. 334+ suspected cases since 2019 per Doctors Without Borders. A tourist was left with 2% vision after one cocktail in Bali 2025. Deaths reported. One shot of methanol can be fatal. ONLY drink sealed beer, wine, or duty-free spirits you opened yourself. Avoid cocktails and shots at unlicensed venues.

reports.title: Diamond Beach and Kelingking Beach swimming deaths
reports.place: Diamond Beach & Kelingking Beach, Nusa Penida
reports.desc: Both beaches are official no-swim zones but tourists repeatedly ignore signs. Strong unpredictable undercurrents and deceptively high waves. Indian tourist Nilesh Mukhi (59) swept away at Angel's Billabong Oct 2024 and found dead. Tourist drowned at Diamond Beach Aug 2025 despite rescue attempts. Don't enter the water.

reports.title: Angel's Billabong wave sweeps tourists off rocks
reports.desc: Waves appear small but crash violently over the volcanic ledge. At least 4 people have drowned here. Indian tourist swept to death 16 Oct 2024. Viral video Sep 2025 shows a group of girls nearly washed away. Take photos from a safe distance; never stand near the water's edge regardless of conditions.

reports.title: Seminyak and Kuta beach riptides — no lifeguard warning
reports.desc: Bali's most popular beaches are largely unpatrolled. A local father drowned at Seminyak 3 Aug 2025 trying to save a child swept away. Australian Craig Stewart (56) drowned 18 Oct 2024 saving a German tourist at Balian Beach. Only swim at patrolled sections with flags. Red flag = no swimming.

reports.title: Gojek/Grab driver demands extra cash off-app
reports.place: Bali-wide, especially Canggu, Ubud, airport routes
reports.desc: After booking via the app, the driver contacts you to cancel in-app and pay cash directly — at a higher price. Removes trip tracking and price protection. Some demand Rp 450k for Rp 200k rides. Only pay the in-app amount. If a driver demands more on arrival, screenshot the booking and dispute via the app.

reports.title: Grab/Gojek banned in tourist zones — forced into local taxis
reports.place: Uluwatu, Canggu main spots, Ubud, Sanur/Padangbai ports
reports.desc: Local taxi cooperatives ('mafias') prohibit app-based pickups in multiple tourist hotspots. Tourists exiting Uluwatu temple, the Canggu main strip, or the ferry ports get forced into unregulated overpriced local transport. Negotiate price firmly before boarding or walk to the zone boundary to use apps.

reports.title: Fake visa and e-VOA websites overcharging tourists
reports.place: Online — Google search results and Instagram ads
reports.desc: Unofficial websites rank high in search for 'Bali visa' and the Indonesia arrivals card and charge 2–5× the official price. One tourist's girlfriend had account details stolen mid-application. Official Indonesian government sites end with .go.id only. Pay the Bali tourist levy (Rp 150k) only at lovebali.baliprov.go.id.

reports.title: Timeshare 'free prize' holiday presentation trap
reports.place: Shopping streets in Kuta, Seminyak; staff approach on foot
reports.desc: Approached with an offer of free gift, scratch card, or 'you've been selected' prize. Must attend a 'short presentation' at a resort or office. Karma Group-style timeshare pitch that wastes half your day and may pressure you into deposits. Politely decline all unsolicited 'prize' offers immediately.

reports.title: Airport porter overcharging for luggage help
reports.place: Ngurah Rai Airport — arrivals exit to taxi area
reports.desc: Unofficial porters grab your bags at the exit and demand Rp 50k per bag. Official rate if you use a porter is Rp 20k per bag. Free trolleys are available — use them yourself. If you accept porter help, agree the rate before they touch your bags.

reports.title: 'New from Saudi Arabia' currency-swap wallet-check distraction
reports.place: Nusa Dua Beach Road; also reported in Ubud
reports.desc: A couple in a car stop pedestrians, claim to be new arrivals from Saudi Arabia, show foreign banknotes and ask to see local currency. One reaches and presses a hand toward the wallet to gauge contents. Reported twice in Feb 2025 by travellers. Ignore all unsolicited currency-exchange approaches.

reports.title: Dollar / rupiah price ambiguity bait-and-switch
reports.place: Ubud market, Kuta beach vendors, Seminyak shops
reports.desc: Vendor quotes '10' for an item. When you confirm if it's rupiah, they nod. After the item is wrapped, they demand $10 USD. Makes you feel embarrassed to argue. Legal grey area — they 'said 10'. Point out the misunderstanding firmly and walk away. You owe nothing.

reports.title: Driver-commissioned restaurant and shop detour
reports.desc: Private drivers take you to specific restaurants, souvenir shops and art galleries they have commission deals with. Prices inside are 30–50% inflated. Driver insists it's 'the best place'. Plan your own itinerary before departure, name specific places, and refuse unplanned stops firmly.

reports.title: Fake 'hotel front desk' call asking for credit card details
reports.place: Hotels across Bali, especially budget Kuta/Legian
reports.desc: Scammer calls your room posing as hotel front desk, claims a detail in your booking is missing and asks to 'verify' your credit card number over the phone. Real hotel staff will never do this. Hang up and visit reception in person if you have any concern.

reports.title: Tout sells ferry tickets at marked-up price
reports.place: Sanur Port and Padangbai ferry terminal
reports.desc: Gacong guides approach before the official ticket counter and sell tickets at a marked-up price, then walk you to the official counter and pocket the difference. Only buy at the official counter directly. Ignore all approaches from touts before you reach the desk.

reports.title: Roadside petrol overcharge in repurposed vodka bottles
reports.place: Remote roads — Ubud area, Amed, North Bali
reports.desc: Roadside petrol sold in repurposed Absolut vodka bottles at 2–3× official pump price, targeting tourists who think official stations are far away. Many Pertamina (official) stations exist islandwide. Pay exact cash — change manipulation is common. Never pre-pay for an unknown amount.

reports.title: Nusa Penida thieves targeting tourists at viewpoints
reports.desc: The Bali Sun reported Nov 2025: thieves specifically targeting tourists at Nusa Penida's popular sites. Bags, phones, and cameras stolen. Leave valuables in locked accommodation. Use phone neck straps. Don't leave bags unattended for even a moment at crowded viewpoints.
